$comment: Baked USPSTF preventive-screening dataset (issue #149) read by lib/preventive-catalog.ts. Curated PUBLIC USPSTF grade A/B recommendations — see scripts/gen-screenings.ts for per-row sourcing. Committed + HUMAN-REVIEWABLE; regenerate with `npm run gen:screenings`. SIMPLIFIED and INFORMATIONAL for personal/household tracking, NOT clinical software or medical advice. `reviewed` dates the whole dataset; risk-gated rows stay inert until the risk input exists.

citation.summary: Screen adults 18+ for hypertension; frequency varies by age and prior readings (annual default used here).

screenings.name: Cholesterol (lipid) screening
screenings.description: Blood lipid panel to assess cardiovascular risk.

citation.summary: Lipid screening supports statin-use decisions in adults ~40–75; broader periodic screening is common (every ~5 years used here).

screenings.name: Colorectal cancer screening
screenings.description: Screening for colorectal cancer (e.g. colonoscopy or stool-based test). Interval depends on the test used.

citation.summary: Screen adults 45–75 for colorectal cancer; 76–85 is individualized. Multiple strategies (colonoscopy ~10y, annual stool-based, etc.).

screenings.name: Diabetes / prediabetes screening
screenings.description: Blood glucose or A1c screening for type 2 diabetes and prediabetes. USPSTF targets overweight/obese adults.

citation.summary: Screen adults 35–70 who are overweight or obese for prediabetes and type 2 diabetes, about every 3 years.

screenings.name: Depression screening
screenings.description: Screening for depression (e.g. a PHQ-2 / PHQ-9 questionnaire) in adolescents and adults.

citation.summary: Screen adolescents 12–18 and adults 18+ (including during pregnancy and postpartum) for depression where follow-up care is available. Optimal rescreening interval is not established (annual default used here).

screenings.name: Hepatitis C screening
screenings.description: One-time blood test for hepatitis C infection.

citation.summary: Screen adults 18–79 for hepatitis C infection at least once.

screenings.name: Cervical cancer screening
screenings.description: Cervical cancer screening (Pap cytology and/or HPV testing). Interval depends on the test used.

citation.summary: Screen women 21–65: cytology every 3 years (21–65), or HPV / co-testing every 5 years (30–65).

screenings.name: Mammography (breast cancer screening)
screenings.description: Breast cancer screening with mammography, typically every 2 years.

citation.summary: Screen women 65+ for osteoporosis with bone measurement testing. The optimal rescreening interval is uncertain.

screenings.name: Lung cancer screening (low-dose CT)
screenings.description: Annual low-dose CT for adults 50–80 with a significant smoking history. Requires smoking history — inactive until that is recorded.

citation.summary: Annual low-dose CT for adults 50–80 with a 20 pack-year history who currently smoke or quit within 15 years.

screenings.name: Abdominal aortic aneurysm screening
screenings.description: One-time ultrasound for men 65–75 who have ever smoked. Requires smoking history — inactive until that is recorded.

citation.summary: One-time abdominal ultrasound for men 65–75 who have ever smoked.
